channel.title: SevenAtSevenSection1
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenSection1, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: In a world built for sitting, here's how to stay active — even when stuck inside
item.description: In the office, classroom and living room, working and relaxing mean sitting still. Our bodies evolved without chairs. Here are some tips for getting o...

item.title: How much power does the Fed chair really have?
item.description: On paper, the Fed chair is just one vote among many. In practice, the job carries far more influence. We analyze what gives the Fed chair power.

item.title: DHS faces funding deadline. And, courts fast-track Somali asylum seeker hearings
item.description: Congress has until Friday to reach a deal to fund the Department of Homeland Security. And, several asylum cases filed by Somali migrants in immigrati...

item.title: An ape, a tea party — and the ability to imagine
item.description: The ability to imagine — to play pretend — has long been thought to be unique to humans. A new study suggests one of our closest living relatives can ...

item.title: This complex brain network may explain many of Parkinson's stranger symptoms
item.description: Parkinson's disease appears to disrupt a brain network involved in everything from movement to memory.

item.title: Tem raises $75M to remake electricity markets using AI
item.description: Tem has built a transaction engine that relies on AI to cut costs. Lightspeed led the round to help the startup expand to the U.S. and Australia.

item.title: The first signs of burnout are coming from the people who embrace AI the most
item.description: Because employees could do more, work began bleeding into lunch breaks and late evenings. The employees' to-do lists expanded to fill every hour that ...

item.title: 2026’s Top Growing Cities (People Are Moving Here!)
item.description: The “lock-in effect” is finally starting to break, and Americans are moving yet again. But, where are they going? The top cities people are moving to ...

item.title: India makes Aadhaar more ubiquitous, but critics say security and privacy concerns remain
item.description: India's Aadhaar is moving into wallets, hotels and policing through a new app. Critics say that amid the broader Aadhaar rollout, it's unclear how dat...

item.title: Trump’s immigration chiefs are set to testify in Congress following protester deaths
item.description: The heads of agencies enforcing President Donald Trump’s immigration agenda are set to testify before Congress.

item.title: YouTube rolls out an AI playlist generator for Premium users
item.description: YouTube Music is rolling out a new AI-powered playlist-generation feature that allows Premium users on iOS and Android to use text prompts to create p...

item.title: ‘We hold these truths': Viewing the Declaration of Independence, visitors reflect on America at 250
How Americans’ optimism about their future has changed, according to new polling
The Small Sacrifices That Gave Me 25 Rentals and $18,000/Month Cash Flow
item.description: One property can change your entire life. Less than a decade after buying your first, you could be completely financially free, like today’s guest, wh...

item.title: The W2 Employee’s Roadmap to Financial Freedom (Buy Rentals While Working 8-6)
item.description: Think you’re too busy to own rental properties? Real estate investing doesn’t have to dominate your time or energy. Today’s guest is living proof, hav...

item.title: Waymo is testing driverless robotaxis in Nashville
item.description: This is the typical next step before it launches a commercial service.

item.title: The 7 Places Self-Managing Landlords Lose Money Without Realizing It
item.description: This article is presented by RentRedi. When I bought my first rental, I was determined to self-manage. Not because I loved property management, but be...

item.title: A Fast-Growing Renter Demographic is Creating Better Cash Flow Opportunities For Investors
item.description: Solo living is no longer a state enforced on a spouse when their other half passes away, but increasingly a lifestyle choice many Americans prefer or ...
